parsed_review.summary: CP00-934 is a descriptor-only HRX AdminPolicy/SyntheticTenant bridge pack (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false) across RP30.P04.M10.S06-RP30.P05.M00.S06. The pack baseline is fully consistent across manifest, closeout-pack-plan, review-baseline, contract, and registry binding: risk A, 10 units, 4 AdminPolicy rows (RP30.P04.M10.S06-S09) + 6 SyntheticTenant rows (RP30.P05.M00.S01-S06), deliverable {implementation:10}, upstream CP00-933, next CP00-935 / RP30.P05.M00.S07. The descriptor-only claim is supported: HRX source (registry.js, validators.js) contains no writeFile/network/child_process/runtime operations; validateHrxCp934AdminPolicySyntheticTenantBridgeDescriptor strictly enforces every runtime/record-write/rule-execution/finalization/real-data/credential/permission-bypass/fixture-runtime flag to false and asserts descriptor_only/hrx_embedded/domain_bridge true per row, plus baseline-decision, domain-entity, Hermes-packet, and Claude-packet boundary checks. model.test.js covers binding, coverage distribution (P04=4/P05=6), descriptor boundary closure, and contract projection, asserting zero errors. The validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs script validates the full CP897->CP934 binding chain and the contract; required npm scripts exist. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (deferred to CP00-935); Hermes packet is deterministic_only with emits_runtime_receipt=false, used only for review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. README accurately describes the pack without overclaiming, and changed-file-scope correctly isolates the 6 pack files from unrelated UI-track dirty files. Verdict reached by static read-only inspection only (Read/Grep/Glob); I did not re-execute the validation ladder. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 transparency note: command-evidence reflects pack-construction-time runs while the full closeout validation ladder and Hermes control-plane gate remain pending_final_validation and must be completed by the closeout orchestrator (recording exactly one valid review receipt) before commit. Does not block this CP's pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Verdict from static read-only inspection; full validation ladder and Hermes gate remain pending_final_validation
findings.details: command-evidence.json records 5 HRX-specific commands (node --check x3, node --test, npm run rp30:hrx:validate) all at exit_code 0, with status 'pending_final_validation' and hermes_gate.status 'pending' (rp00_control_plane_contract_validate, spec_requirement_validate). The broader closeout validation ladder named in the review baseline (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet captured in artifacts. As a read-only reviewer I confirmed the descriptor evidence, validators, tests, contract, plan, and binding are internally consistent and boundary-closed, but I did not re-execute any command; this PASS rests on static inspection of well-formed, consistent artifacts. This is the expected pre-review state, not a pack defect.
findings.recommendation: Closeout orchestrator must execute the full validation ladder and the Hermes control-plane gate to green, flip the pending construction-inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result records, and record exactly one valid hardened review receipt before committing CP00-934. No change to pack source required.
